UNESCO Criteria: (i) to represent a masterpiece of human creative genius;
Local Stone Name: Makrana Marble (Note: This is historically accurate but missing from the official UNESCO text)
Geological Age: Proterozoic (Delhi Supergroup / Aravalli Supergroup for Makrana Marble); Mesoproterozoic (Vindhyan Supergroup for Red Sandstone)
Geological Age_Ext: (PDF) Rajnagar Marble: a Prominent Heritage Stone from Rajasthan, NW India. (n.d.). ResearchGate. https://www.researchgate.net/publication/356953335_Rajnagar_Marble_a_Prominent_Heritage_Stone_from_Rajasthan_NW_India
Colour: ure white with greyish veins (Marble); Reddish-brown (Sandstone)
Minerals: Calcite (chiefly), with minor quartz and mica
Quarry: Makrana (Nagaur District, Rajasthan) for White Marble; Tantpur/Sikri region for Red Sandstone
Local vs Imported: Imported / Regional Transport (~300 km from Makrana, Rajasthan to Agra)
Transport Distance: ~300 km (from Makrana quarries in Rajasthan to Agra)
Structural Use: Main tomb structure, minarets, platforms, domes, and wall veneering.
Decorative Use: Exquisite octagonal marble lattice screens, pietra dura stone intarsia inlay (using precious/semi-precious stones), floral relief motifs, and Arabic calligraphic inscriptions.
Masonry Technique: Brick-in-lime mortar core veneered with dressed stone slices.
Weathering: Deterioration due to atmospheric pollutants; monitored constantly via an air control monitoring station installed on site
Restoration: Continuous repairs and conservation works conducted since the British period in India and ongoing under Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) management

Construction Period: 13th to 17th Century (with Roman/Etruscan foundation roots dating back to 59 BC)
Secondary Stone: Carrara Marble, White Marble, Prato Serpentinite (Green Marble/Verde di Prato), Plasterwork
Local Stone Name: Pietra Forte, Pietra Serena, Pietra Bigia
Lithology: Fine-grained lithic arenite with calcite cement (Pietra Forte); Medium-to-coarse-grained quartzose-feldspathic greywacke / sandstone (Pietra Serena)
Lithology_Ext: (PDF) The sandstone’s chromatic alteration of the florentine cultural heritage. (2007, January 1). ResearchGate. https://www.researchgate.net/publication/258615958_The_sandstone's_chromatic_alteration_of_the_florentine_cultural_heritage
Geological Age: Upper Cretaceous (Pietra Forte); Upper Oligocene to Lower Miocene (Pietra Serena)
Formation: External Liguride Complex / Pietraforte Formation (Pietra Forte); Macigno Formation / Monte Modino Formation (Pietra Serena)
Formation_Ext: (PDF) The sandstone’s chromatic alteration of the florentine cultural heritage. (2007, January 1). ResearchGate. https://www.researchgate.net/publication/258615958_The_sandstone's_chromatic_alteration_of_the_florentine_cultural_heritage
Colour: Ochre / Yellowish-brown when weathered (Pietra Forte); Bluish-grey fresh, oxidizing to ochre/reddish-brown (Pietra Serena)
Minerals: Quartz, Feldspar, Micas (Biotite), Calcite cement, Clay matrix (Illite, Kaolinite, Chlorite)
Quarry: Fiesole ridge (Monte Ceceri, Settignano, Vincigliata), Gonfolina, Carmignano (Pietra Serena); Boboli, Costa San Giorgio, Monteripaldi (Pietra Forte); Apuan Alps / Carrara (Marble)
Local vs Imported: Local (Pietra Forte & Pietra Serena are indigenous Tuscan stones); Regional/Imported (Carrara Marble from Apuan Alps)
Transport Distance: Local (Within < 5 km to 30 km radius surrounding Florence)
Structural Use: Bearing structures, heavy foundation blocks, load-bearing walls, bridge piers, city walls, rustic ashlar facades
Decorative Use: Internal columns, capitals, window frames, lintels, balustrades, relief carving, architectural molding
Masonry Technique: Rusticated ashlar masonry (Bozzi / Bozzette), dressed stone masonry, cobblestone paving, plasterwork combined with dressed stone
Weathering: Exfoliation, peeling, clay swelling, Fe²⁺ to Fe³⁺ iron oxidation causing chromatic alteration (reddish-brown staining), atmospheric pollution decay
Replacement Stone: Firenzuola Sandstone (Pietra di Firenzuola), artificial stone mortars, replacement Pietra Serena
Restoration: Conservative consolidation, emergency flood mitigation measures, plaster and mortar repair, structural conservation practices
